contentBlocks.original:
सुवक्षोजकुम्भां सुधापूर्णकुम्भां
प्रसादावलम्बां प्रपुण्यावलम्बाम् ।
सदास्येन्दुबिम्बां सदानोष्ठबिम्बां
भजे शारदाम्बामजस्रं मदम्बाम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suvakṣōjakumbhāṃ sudhāpūrṇakumbhāṃ
prasādāvalambāṃ prapuṇyāvalambām ।
sadāsyēndubimbāṃ sadānōṣṭhabimbāṃ
bhajē śāradāmbāmajasraṃ madambām ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Śāradā — with beautiful, full breasts, with a pot full of nectar (ambrosia), depending on grace, depending on merit accumulated — with a face like the orb of the moon, with a moon-crescent on the forehead — who illumines and opens the heart-lotus, reddening the lotus of wisdom — I meditate on you.
contentBlocks.meanings: su-vakṣoja-kumbha: beautiful full breasts | sudhā-pūrṇa-kumbha: pot full of ambrosia/nectar | prasāda-avalamba: depending on grace | prapuṇya-avalamba: depending on accumulated merit | sadā-āsya-indu-bimba: face ever like the moon-orb | candra-kalā: moon-crescent | hṛt-padma-bodha: awakening the heart-lotus | rakta-ambuja-śrī: reddening the lotus of wisdom

contentBlocks.original:
कटाक्षे दयार्द्रां करे ज्ञानमुद्रां
कलाभिर्विनिद्रां कलापैः सुभद्राम् ।
पुरस्त्रीं विनिद्रां पुरस्तुङ्गभद्रां
भजे शारदाम्बामजस्रं मदम्बाम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaṭākṣē dayārdrāṃ karē jñānamudrāṃ
kalābhirvinidrāṃ kalāpaiḥ subhadrām ।
purastrīṃ vinidrāṃ purastuṅgabhadrāṃ
bhajē śāradāmbāmajasraṃ madambām ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whose sidelong glance is moist with compassion — whose hand is in the gesture of knowledge (jñāna-mudrā) — who is ever-awake through her arts — who is supremely auspicious through her multifarious arts — who is a deity in human female form (pura-strī) — who is supremely honored — I bow to Śāradā.
contentBlocks.meanings: kaṭākṣe dayārdra: sidelong glance moist with compassion | kare jñāna-mudrā: hand in knowledge-gesture | kalābhir vinidra: ever-awake through arts | kalāpaiḥ subhadra: auspicious through arts | pura-strī: deity in female form | vicitra: supremely honored

contentBlocks.original:
ललामाङ्कफालां लसद्गानलोलां
स्वभक्तैकपालां यशःश्रीकपोलाम् ।
करे त्वक्षमालां कनत्पत्रलोलां
भजे शारदाम्बामजस्रं मदम्बाम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lalāmāṅkaphālāṃ lasadgānalōlāṃ
svabhaktaikapālāṃ yaśaḥśrīkapōlām ।
karē tvakṣamālāṃ kanatpatralōlāṃ
bhajē śāradāmbāmajasraṃ madambām ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With forehead adorned by a beautiful mark (tilaka) — eager/playful in shining song (gāna) — the sole protectress of her devotees — with cheeks radiating fame and prosperity — with a rosary in her hand — with gleaming leaf-earrings — riding a wild peacock — salutations to Śāradā.
contentBlocks.meanings: lalāma-aṅka-phālā: forehead with beautiful mark | lasat-gāna-lolā: eager/playful in shining song | sva-bhakta-eka-pālā: sole protectress of devotees | yaśaḥ-śrī-kapolā: cheeks radiating fame and prosperity | kare tvak-ṣamālā: rosary in hand | kanat-patralā: gleaming leaf-earrings | mayūra-ādhirūḍhā: riding a peacock

contentBlocks.original:
सुसीमन्तवेणीं दृशा निर्जितैणीं
रमत्कीरवाणीं नमद्वज्रपाणीम् ।
सुधामन्थरास्यां मुदा चिन्त्यवेणीं
भजे शारदाम्बामजस्रं मदम्बाम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
susīmantavēṇīṃ dṛśā nirjitaiṇīṃ
ramatkīravāṇīṃ namadvajrapāṇīm ।
sudhāmantharāsyāṃ mudā chintyavēṇīṃ
bhajē śāradāmbāmajasraṃ madambām ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: With a beautiful central parting in her hair — whose eyes defeat those of a doe in beauty — whose voice delights in parrots (or: whose parrot-like voice delights) — who is bowed to by the Vajra-handed (Indra) — with a moon-like leisurely face — with mind ever directed to the auspicious — I praise Śāradā.
contentBlocks.meanings: su-sīmanta-veṇī: hair with beautiful central parting | dṛśā nirjita-aiṇī: eyes defeating those of a doe | ramat-kīra-vāṇī: voice delighting in parrots (or parrot-like voice) | namat-vajra-pāṇī: bowed to by Indra (Vajrapāṇi) | sudhā-manthara-āsyā: face with moon-like leisure | mudā cinta-maṅgala: mind always thinking of the auspicious

contentBlocks.original:
सुशान्तां सुदेहां दृगन्ते कचान्तां
लसत्सल्लताङ्गीमनन्तामचिन्त्याम् ।
स्मरेत्तापसैः सर्गपूर्वस्थितां तां
भजे शारदाम्बामजस्रं मदम्बाम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suśāntāṃ sudēhāṃ dṛgantē kachāntāṃ
lasatsallatāṅgīmanantāmachintyām ।
smarēttāpasaiḥ sargapūrvasthitāṃ tāṃ
bhajē śāradāmbāmajasraṃ madambām ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Supremely peaceful — of supremely beautiful body — at the corner of whose eyes the hair reaches (or: whose eyes reach the hair's edge) — with a body of beautiful creeper-like grace — infinite, unthinkable — meditated upon by ascetics — salutations to Śāradā, the omnipresent one.
contentBlocks.meanings: su-śāntā: supremely peaceful | su-dehā: supremely beautiful body | dṛgante kacāntā: at the corner of the eyes, to the hair's edge | lasat-sallataṅgī: body of beautiful creeper-like grace | anantā: infinite | acintā: unthinkable | smaret-tāpasaiḥ: meditated upon by ascetics | sarvagā: omnipresent

contentBlocks.original:
कुरङ्गे तुरङ्गे मृगेन्द्रे खगेन्द्रे
मराले मदेभे महोक्षेऽधिरूढाम् ।
महत्यां नवम्यां सदा सामरूपां
भजे शारदाम्बामजस्रं मदम्बाम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kuraṅgē turaṅgē mṛgēndrē khagēndrē
marālē madēbhē mahōkṣē'dhirūḍhām ।
mahatyāṃ navamyāṃ sadā sāmarūpāṃ
bhajē śāradāmbāmajasraṃ madambām ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who has been mounted upon a deer, a horse, the king of beasts (lion), the king of birds (eagle), a goose, an intoxicated elephant, and a great ox — on the great ninth day (Mahānavamī) — who is worshipped on that occasion — salutations to Śāradā.
contentBlocks.meanings: kuraṅge: upon a deer | turaṅge: upon a horse | mṛgendre: upon the king of beasts (lion) | khagendre: upon the king of birds (Garuḍa/eagle) | marāle: upon a goose | mad-ebhe: upon an intoxicated elephant | mahokṣe: upon a great ox | adhirūḍhā: mounted upon | mahānavamī: the great ninth day (Navamī of Navarātri) | pūjitā: worshipped

contentBlocks.original:
ज्वलत्कान्तिवह्निं जगन्मोहनाङ्गीं
भजे मानसाम्भोज सुभ्रान्तभृङ्गीम् ।
निजस्तोत्रसङ्गीतनृत्यप्रभाङ्गीं
भजे शारदाम्बामजस्रं मदम्बाम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jvalatkāntivahniṃ jaganmōhanāṅgīṃ
bhajē mānasāmbhōja subhrāntabhṛṅgīm ।
nijastōtrasaṅgītanṛtyaprabhāṅgīṃ
bhajē śāradāmbāmajasraṃ madambām ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who blazes with radiant, dazzling light — with a world-enchanting body — I worship her who is like a wildly wandering bee in the lotus of the mind. Who is accompanied by her own praise-songs — who is honored by those who compose devotional poems — who is the Mother of all knowledge — I worship Śāradā.
contentBlocks.meanings: jvalat-kānti-vahni: blazing with dazzling radiance/fire | jagat-mohana-aṅgī: world-enchanting body | mānasa-ambhoja-su-bhrānta-bhṛṅgī: wildly wandering bee in the mind-lotus | nija-stotra-saṅgī: accompanied by her own praise-songs | stotrā-pūjitā: honored by those who make stotrams | sarva-vidyā-jananī: mother of all knowledge

contentBlocks.original:
भवाम्भोजनेत्राजसम्पूज्यमानां
लसन्मन्दहासप्रभावक्त्रचिह्नाम् ।
चलच्चञ्चलाचारुताटङ्ककर्णां
भजे शारदाम्बामजस्रं मदम्बाम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavāmbhōjanētrājasampūjyamānāṃ
lasanmandahāsaprabhāvaktrachihnām ।
chalachchañchalāchārutāṭaṅkakarṇāṃ
bhajē śāradāmbāmajasraṃ madambām ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Supremely worshipped by Brahmā of the lotus-eyes, with the mark of a gentle smile on her moon-like luminous face — with swaying, graceful, beautiful ear-ornaments — who holds in her hands the vīṇā and books — salutations to Śāradā, who grants the wished-for blessings.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhava-ambhoja-neta-āja: Brahmā of the lotus (padma/bhava = Brahmā) | sampūjyamāna: supremely worshipped | lasat-manda-hāsa-prabhā-vaktra-cihna: mark of gentle smile on luminous face | calat-cañcalā-cāru-tāṭaṅka-kara: swaying, graceful, beautiful ear-ornaments | vīṇā-pustaka-dhāriṇī: bearer of vīṇā and books | abhīṣṭa-dāyinī: granter of wished-for blessings

contentBlocks.original: ॥ इति श्रीमच्छङ्कराचार्यविरचिता श्री शारदा भुजङ्ग प्रयाताष्टकम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: ॥ iti śrīmachChaṅkarāchāryavirachitā śrī śāradā bhujaṅga prayātāṣṭakam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Śāradā Bhujanga Prayāta Aṣṭakam composed by Śrī Śaṅkarācārya.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti: thus ends | śrī śaṅkarācārya-viracitā: composed by Śrī Śaṅkarācārya | śrī śāradā bhujanga prayāta aṣṭakam: the Śāradā Bhujanga Prayāta Aṣṭakam (eight-stanza hymn in the serpent-gliding meter)
